After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS VANESSA MEANS AND BRETT MEANS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact exist including but not limited to the scope of the release, which is supplemented by an ambiguous hand-written term; landlord's good faith intent to move-in and landlord's legitimate bona fide reason for failure to move in within 3 months. It is defendant's burden to assert the existence of the release as an affirmative defense and to show that the release covers the claims asserted by Plaintiff. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that it is Plaintiff's burden to seek reformation of the settlement agreement. Furthermore, defendant failed to shift its burden and establish that Plaintiff's causes of action stemming from violation of OMI provisions (last element of the cause of action being failure to timely move-in that have not yet occurred at the time of the settlement), was a known, then-existing claim at the time of the execution of the settlement agreement subject to the release (as opposed to release of all unknown claims). Plaintiff to prepare order in accordance with this ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Stephanie Bryant, CSR # 13160, stephaniebryantCSR@gmail.com, 619-997-1077. (D501)
